mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and increased recession risk, which compresses equity risk premiums and reduces expected corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals robust aggregate demand and expanding corporate profit margins, driving positive equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 23548.21 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2025-01-01
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with quarterly GDPC1 growth rates (Spearman rho < 0.05), or positive GDP growth is followed by negative SPX returns in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening consumer demand and construction activity, indicating reduced near-term corporate revenue growth and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls > 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 714568.0 millions as of 2025-05-01
fact:Current HOUST at 1289.0 thousands as of 2025-05-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.02 when tested independently against single-series alternatives over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization, compressing profit margins and earnings expectations, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 100.9655 index (2017=100) as of 2025-05-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating payroll employment growth combined with rising inflation signals tight labor markets and wage-price spiral risk, which increases corporate cost pressures and reduces profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 250k month-over-month AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 158478 thousands as of 2025-06-01
fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 321.435 index as of 2025-06-01
regime_hint: late-cycle inflation with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal shows lower predictive power than either component alone (Rank IC difference > 0.01 in favor of single series).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3% annualized while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, the real policy rate turns negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 321.435 index as of 2025-06-01
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in negative real rate regimes are not significantly higher than positive real rate regimes (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction demand and residential investment; sustained HOUST above 1.3 million units signals robust housing demand and forward economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3 million units annualized for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1289.0 thousands as of 2025-05-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sustained HOUST > 1.3M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
